description_long: This is a pair of hand pliers featuring two elongated, rectangular handles coated in a yellow material, likely for insulation and improved grip. The handles are joined by a central black metal pivot, which allows the jaws to open and close smoothly. The jaws themselves are short and robust, with a flat gripping surface and a small notch near the tip, suitable for gripping, bending, or cutting wire. The yellow handles display a printed logo and some text, indicating branding and possibly model information. The overall construction suggests durability and ergonomic use, with the handles designed to fit comfortably in the hand. The pliers are compact and practical, commonly found in toolboxes for urban maintenance, electrical work, or general repairs.
description: A compact pair of yellow-handled pliers with a black metal pivot and short, flat jaws, featuring a visible logo and ergonomic design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the yellow handles and the black metal jaws, with the logo and text visible on the upper handle.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the rectangular cross-section of the handles and the pivot joint, with the jaws partially obscured.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other side of the handles and the pivot, with the jaw tips visible and the logo text reversed.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the open jaws and the inside surfaces of the handles, with the pivot mechanism clearly visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, rectangular, insulated handles with a yellow plastic or rubber coating for ergonomic grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Short, flat jaws with a small notch near the tip, suitable for gripping and cutting; lacks serration or needle-nose features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed single pivot joint, allowing standard opening and closing motion without adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth yellow coating with no visible ribbing or knurling, providing a basic non-slip surface.
